name: Las Tres Marías Trail
name_original: Valle de Arbás – circular desde Casares de Arbás (Reserva de la Biosfera Alto Bernesga)
description: Hike the moderate Las Tres Marías Trail, a 7.0-mile route in Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga, featuring forest and mountain views.
region_intro: Hiking around Villamanín offers access to the mountainous terrain of the Cantabrian Mountains in León, Spain. The region is characterized by significant altitude variations, with numerous peaks exceeding 2,000 meters, including Brañacaballo. Within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, the Valle de La Tercia features green fields, clear rivers, and dense pine forests, providing diverse landscapes for exploration. Best hiking trails around Villamanín The most popular hiking route is Las Tres Marías Trail, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 32 minutes to complete. This route offers captivating forest and mountain views within the Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico Brañacaballo, Alto del Sexteo, Pico de la Foya y Pico de la Pizarra – circular desde Millaró de la Tercia, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This route includes a significant elevation gain, leading to panoramic mountain vistas. Local hikers also love the Cuitu Negru Loop Trail, a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with panoramic views, often completed in about 5 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Villamanín is defined by mountainous terrain, the scenic Valle de La Tercia, and dense pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Villamanín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Villamanín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Villamanín?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails around Villamanín, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and interests. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Cantabrian Mountains and the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Villamanín?
A: Hiking in Villamanín offers dramatic mountainous terrain, with numerous peaks exceeding 2,000 meters. You'll encounter lush green fields in areas like the Valle de La Tercia, clear rivers and streams, and dense pine forests, all contributing to picturesque backdrops.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes in Villamanín?
A: Yes, Villamanín offers several challenging hikes for experienced trekkers. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239840155/cuitu-negru-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuitu Negru Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.7 km (9.8-mile) difficult trail that features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. There are 7 difficult routes in total.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly hiking options in Villamanín?
A: Yes, Villamanín has several easy hiking options suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. There are 9 easy routes available, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty without extensive physical demands.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Villamanín?
A: Yes, many routes in Villamanín are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1239840155/cuitu-negru-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuitu Negru Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051400154/pico-branacaballo-alto-del-sexteo-pico-de-la-foya-y-pico-de-la-pizarra-circular-desde-millaro-de-la-tercia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Brañacaballo, Alto del Sexteo, Pico de la Foya y Pico de la Pizarra – circular desde Millaró de la Tercia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints along the hiking trails in Villamanín?
A: The region offers stunning vistas. You can find routes with views of Las Tres Marías from locations like Collada Gistreo, or from Cubillas de Arbás. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151883" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coto Bello Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot for panoramic scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Villamanín?
A: The hiking trails in Villamanín are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 120 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the scenic beauty of the Valle de La Tercia, and the dense pine forests that characterize the routes.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks to see near the trails?
A: Yes, the area around Villamanín is rich in natural attractions. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;, traverse the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5558242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Manadero in the Cave of the Doves&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Villamanín?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Villamanín area, especially within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Villamanín?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Villamanín are typically spring, summer, and early autumn. During these months, the weather is generally more favorable, with pleasant temperatures and less chance of snow at higher altitudes, allowing full access to the mountainous trails and lush landscapes.

Q: Are there any historical routes in the Villamanín area?
A: Yes, the region is crossed by the historic Ruta Vía de la Plata (Silver Route), a long-distance hiking route that follows an ancient Roman road. This offers a unique blend of natural beauty and historical significance for hikers.
tips: Fantastic views of the 3 Marías and on the other side, the Casares reservoir. An authentic viewpoint in the middle of the road.
We are in the Collada Gistreo, located at an altitude of 1634 meters, from where we have perfect views of the Tres Marías and the entire valley.
Picturesque path under the shade of a dense pine forest. Passage area of the Casares de Arbás route.

faqs.question: Where does the Las Tres Marías Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location. While the exact trailhead isn't specified in the data, it is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga. Look for parking options near the starting point, likely in a nearby village or designated area within the reserve.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Las Tres Marías Trail is rated as 'moderate' difficulty. It involves an elevation gain of approximately 459 meters over 11.2 kilometers. While not extremely difficult, it requires a reasonable level of fitness due to its length and elevation changes. Beginners with some hiking experience should be able to manage it.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several points of interest. You'll find a beautiful View of Las Tres Marías, pass through the Collada Gistreo (1,634 m), and experience a serene Pine Forest Near Collado de Gistreo. These highlights provide diverse scenery and memorable spots along your hike.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike in the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga generally depends on weather conditions. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring snow, making some sections more challenging or inaccessible. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Las Tres Marías Trail?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga can vary. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Check specific signage at the trailhead or the reserve's official website for the most current rules.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the trail or the Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga, it's important to be aware of any specific regulations. Generally, access to biosphere reserves for hiking is free, but certain activities or areas might require permits. It's recommended to check the official website of the reserve for any specific permit requirements or fees before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Las Tres Marías Trail?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details for this particular route are not provided, a moderate hike in a biosphere reserve typically involves well-defined paths, which may include dirt tracks, rocky sections, and potentially some forest trails. Given the elevation changes, expect some uphill and downhill sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route for the Las Tres Marías Trail also passes through sections of the Sendero de las Tres Marías (100% of the route), the Transcantábrica (48% of the route), and the Puente del Glcaciar (4% of the route).

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: As part of the Reserva de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga, the area is rich in biodiversity. While specific sightings are not guaranteed, you might encounter various bird species, small mammals, and diverse flora typical of mountain environments in northern Spain. Always observe wildlife from a distance and do not disturb their natural habitat.

faqs.question: Is the Las Tres Marías Trail a loop hike?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Las Tres Marías Trail is a circular route, meaning it forms a loop. You will start and finish your hike at the same point, offering a continuous journey through the landscape without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length and elevation, wear comfortable, sturdy hiking boots and appropriate layered clothing suitable for the weather conditions. Bring enough water, snacks, a map or GPS device (like komoot), sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A backpack to carry your essentials is also recommended.

waypoints.name: Pine Forest Near Collado de Gistreo
